title: Aeva Technologies (AEVA) Launches AI-Powered CityOS Platform for Intelligent Transportation Systems
content:
Aeva Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEVA) is one of the 10 Fastest Growing Edge Computing Stocks to Buy Now. On March 17, 2026, Aeva Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEVA) announced the launch of Aeva CityOS, an AI-powered Intelligent Transportation Systems platform built on Nvidia’s AGX Orin. The company said the platform is designed to digitize intersections and roadways, enabling real-time traffic intelligence to improve safety, efficiency, and urban planning through data-driven insights.

Last month, Aeva Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEVA) reported Q4 adjusted EPS of (40c), ahead of the (44c) consensus estimate. Revenue came in at $5.6M versus the $3.6M consensus. CEO Soroush Salehian described 2025 as a “landmark year,” citing growing demand for the company’s perception platform across multiple applications. He highlighted the company’s first series production award with a top 10 passenger OEM, a new development program with a top 5 OEM, and an initial defense win with Forterra for autonomous ground vehicles, adding that momentum is expected to continue into 2026.Aeva Technologies (AEVA) Launches AI-Powered CityOS Platform for Intelligent Transportation Systems

Copyright: microolga / 123RF Stock Photo

Aeva Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEVA) expects FY26 revenue of $30M–$36M, above the $28.34M consensus estimate.

Aeva Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEVA) develops LiDAR sensing systems and perception software for autonomous and intelligent systems.

While we acknowledge the potential of AEVA as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: AEVA's CityOS Turns Traffic Smart With AI and LiDAR Tech
content:
Aeva Technologies AEVA has introduced CityOS, an AI-powered traffic platform designed to deliver real-time insights. Built on NVIDIA’s NVDA AGX Orin, the system aims to make intersections and roadways smarter, improving safety, traffic flow and planning. By turning physical infrastructure into intelligent systems, CityOS creates a scalable solution for modern mobility, with NVIDIA’s edge computing playing a key role in enabling real-time decision-making.

At its core, CityOS shifts infrastructure from static to continuously aware by combining 4D LiDAR sensors with edge-based AI processing and integrated software. This setup helps the system understand both the position and movement of objects. It uses AEVA Atlas Orion sensors at intersections, with local processing powered by NVIDIA AGX Orin. This NVIDIA-based system allows continuous, high-resolution monitoring of vehicles, pedestrians and cyclists, creating a digital layer over real-world traffic.

CityOS also improves on traditional systems like cameras and radar, which can be limited by lighting or fixed detection zones. In contrast, AEVA’s solution works in all lighting conditions, directly measures speed, and covers entire intersections. With NVIDIA-enabled edge processing delivering fast analytics, cities can support real-time monitoring, detect wrong-way driving, analyze near-misses, and enhance long-term planning.

While AEVA is building a strong position in smart traffic systems, it is not alone in targeting this growing market. Several other companies are also developing technologies to support smarter and safer urban infrastructure.

Beyond AEVA: Other Players in Smart Infrastructure

Innoviz Technologies INVZ is expanding its role in smart infrastructure by bringing automotive-grade LiDAR into city and industrial environments. The company’s InnovizSMART Long-Range LiDAR supports advanced traffic management and security systems with precise 3D detection reaching up to 400 meters, even in challenging conditions. Innoviz Technologies is also seeing strong momentum in perimeter security testing, where its solutions have demonstrated clear performance advantages over leading conventional systems. With reliable accuracy and straightforward deployment, Innoviz Technologies is positioning itself as a key enabler of safer, smarter and more efficient urban infrastructure.

Ouster OUST is also becoming an important force in smart infrastructure, using its AI-powered BlueCity platform to help cities manage traffic more safely and efficiently. The company’s LiDAR-based system combines real-time 3D detection with NVIDIA edge computing, enabling smarter signal timing and reducing congestion across busy intersections. Ouster’s technology is now expanding rapidly, with Utah set to deploy BlueCity at more than 100 intersections after strong results from earlier installations. With its growing network of smart traffic systems, Ouster is positioning itself as a key player in next-generation urban mobility.

Story Continues

AEVA’s Price Performance, Valuation and Estimates

title: Aeva Reveals CityOS, an AI-Powered Platform for Real-Time Traffic Intelligence with NVIDIA AGX
content:
Aeva announced CityOS, an AI-powered Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) platform for real-time traffic intelligence.

New Platform Combines 4D LiDAR Sensors and Edge AI to Improve the Safety and Efficiency of Urban Infrastructure

MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif., March 17, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Aeva® (Nasdaq: AEVA), a leader in next-generation sensing and perception systems, today announced the introduction of Aeva CityOS™, an AI-powered Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) platform for real-time traffic intelligence built on NVIDIA’s AGX Orin. The platform digitizes intersections and roadways to improve safety, efficiency, and data-driven transportation planning in cities worldwide.

CityOS represents a fundamental shift in how cities perceive and manage mobility by combining automotive-grade 4D LiDAR sensors with edge AI processing and market-leading perception and analytics software in collaboration with industry leaders including NVIDIA. It transforms static infrastructure into intelligent, continuously aware systems capable of understanding not just where objects are but how they move and interact.

With CityOS, transportation agencies can now deploy intelligent infrastructure that delivers continuous, high-resolution perception of vehicles, pedestrians, cyclists, and other road users—enabling safer streets, smarter signal timing, and data-driven urban planning.

"As cities confront rising congestion, Vision Zero goals, and increasing multimodal complexity, the need for precise, real-time infrastructure intelligence has never been greater," said Eric Gannaway, Director of Business Development for ITS at Aeva. "By combining 4D LiDAR sensors with edge AI, CityOS enables cities to understand movement with precision, detect and remove risk before accidents occur, and optimize traffic flow in real time."

From Passive Sensors to Comprehensive Intelligent Transportation Systems

Traditional intelligent transportation systems rely on cameras, radar, or inductive loop sensors that depend on ambient lighting, provide limited spatial detail, or detect only an object’s presence within fixed zones. CityOS introduces a new intelligent architecture built on Aeva’s 4D LiDAR technology, equipping each intersection with Aeva Atlas™ Orion sensors together with perception and analytics for full coverage and comprehensive situational awareness.

Rather than replacing existing infrastructure, CityOS enhances it with high-resolution 4D perception that:

Operates in all lighting conditions: including direct sun glare and pitch darkness. Measures instant velocity directly: like radar, but with dramatically higher camera-like resolution. Provides full intersection coverage: beyond the stop-bar detection zones of inductive loops. Enables precise object classification and trajectory tracking: not just presence detection. Preserves privacy by design: without reliance on cameras that capture personally identifiable information. Future-proofs intersections: by providing valuable insights for V2X communication platforms.

Story Continues

Real-Time Intelligence at the Edge

CityOS performs local edge processing of 4D LiDAR data using the NVIDIA AGX Orin platform, delivering low-latency AI inference without dependence on cloud connectivity. AI-powered perception software continuously detects and classifies road users, while advanced analytics software powers dashboards, alerts, and reporting tools for traffic engineers and operators. The result is a scalable intelligence layer that turns raw perception data into actionable operational insight.

CityOS enables applications including:

Vulnerable road user safety analytics Wrong-way driving detection Real-time traffic flow monitoring Near-miss and conflict analysis Intersection performance optimization Long-term mobility and infrastructure planning A scalable platform for the future of smart cities

CityOS is available today for deployment across urban corridors, intersections, and transportation networks worldwide. For more information about CityOS, visit aeva.com/cityos.

About Aeva Technologies, Inc. (Nasdaq: AEVA)

Aeva’s mission is to bring the next wave of perception to a broad range of applications from automated driving, manufacturing automation and smart infrastructure, to robotics and consumer devices. Aeva is accelerating autonomy with its groundbreaking perception platform that integrates lidar-on-chip technology, system-on-chip processing, and perception algorithms onto silicon leveraging silicon photonics. Aeva 4D LiDAR sensors uniquely detect velocity and position simultaneously, allowing automated devices like vehicles and robots to make more intelligent and safe decisions. For more information, visit www.aeva.com, or connect with us on X or LinkedIn.

Aeva, the Aeva logo, Aeva 4D LiDAR, Aeva Atlas, Aeries, Aeva Eve, Aeva Omni, Aeva CityOS, Aeva Ultra Resolution, Aeva CoreVision, and Aeva X1 are trademarks/registered trademarks of Aeva, Inc. All rights reserved. Third-party trademarks are the property of their respective owners.

title: Aeva to Ring the Nasdaq Closing Bell in Celebration of Its Fifth Year Anniversary as a Public Company
content:
MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif., March 11, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Aeva® (Nasdaq: AEVA), a leader in next-generation sensing and perception systems, today announced that it will ring the Nasdaq Closing Bell on March 17, 2026 at the Nasdaq MarketSite in Times Square.

The Closing Bell ceremony is in celebration of Aeva’s fifth year anniversary as a public company and will be led by Soroush Salehian, Co-founder and CEO at Aeva, and Mina Rezk, Co-founder and CTO at Aeva.

"We are excited to celebrate this milestone as we continue to grow Aeva’s leadership position in next-generation perception enabled by our differentiated perception platform," said Soroush Salehian, Co-founder and CEO at Aeva. "From automotive to factory automation, smart infrastructure and defense, Aeva is expanding into new markets to meet the increasing demand across a diverse range of physical AI applications, and we look forward to further building on our momentum in the years to come."

The Closing Bell ceremony will be broadcast live beginning at 3:45 p.m. EDT and streamed across Nasdaq’s official channels. To view the live broadcast, visit https://www.nasdaq.com/marketsite/bell-ringing-ceremony.

About Aeva Technologies, Inc. (Nasdaq: AEVA)

Aeva’s mission is to bring the next wave of perception to a broad range of applications from automated driving, manufacturing automation and smart infrastructure, to robotics and consumer devices. Aeva is accelerating autonomy with its groundbreaking perception platform that integrates lidar-on-chip technology, system-on-chip processing, and perception algorithms onto silicon leveraging silicon photonics. Aeva 4D LiDAR sensors uniquely detect velocity and position simultaneously, allowing automated devices like vehicles and robots to make more intelligent and safe decisions. For more information, visit www.aeva.com, or connect with us on X or LinkedIn.

Aeva, the Aeva logo, Aeva 4D LiDAR, Aeva Atlas, Aeries, Aeva Eve, Aeva Omni, Aeva Ultra Resolution, Aeva CoreVision, and Aeva X1 are trademarks/registered trademarks of Aeva, Inc. All rights reserved. Third-party trademarks are the property of their respective owners.

title: Aeva Technologies Inc (AEVA) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights: Record Revenue and Strategic ...
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Release Date: February 26, 2026

For the complete transcript of the earnings call, please refer to the full earnings call transcript.

Positive Points

Aeva Technologies Inc (NASDAQ:AEVA) doubled its revenue in 2025, reaching a new record for the company. The company secured a major passenger vehicle production program award from a top European OEM, marking a significant milestone. Nvidia selected Aeva Technologies Inc (NASDAQ:AEVA) as the reference LiDAR sensor for its Drive Hyperion platform, enhancing its credibility in the industry. Aeva Technologies Inc (NASDAQ:AEVA) formed a strategic collaboration with LG Innotech, which includes a $50 million investment to expand into physical AI applications. The company successfully reduced its non-GAAP operating expenses by 12% in 2025, demonstrating financial discipline.

Negative Points

Aeva Technologies Inc (NASDAQ:AEVA) reported a non-GAAP operating loss of $102 million for the full year 2025. The company's gross cash use was $119.7 million for the full year, indicating high cash burn. There is uncertainty regarding the exact timing of customer shipments and development activities, which could impact revenue targets. The competitive landscape in the LiDAR industry remains challenging, with consolidation among capable companies. Aeva Technologies Inc (NASDAQ:AEVA) faces risks associated with transitioning OEMs from time of flight to FMCW technology, which may affect adoption rates.

Q & A Highlights

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 7 Warning Signs with AEVA. Is AEVA fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Q: Can you elaborate on the Nvidia relationship and its potential benefits? A: Sureoush Salahian, CEO: The Nvidia collaboration is significant as it validates LiDAR technology. We've been working with Nvidia for some time, and we're actively involved in a production program for an OEM. This partnership involves integrating our Atlas Ultra into Nvidia's Hyperion Drive platform, which is a common platform for OEMs. This could lead to significant opportunities as multiple OEMs adopt this technology for Level 3 and beyond automation.

Q: Regarding the TOP5 passenger OEM development program, what were the competitive dynamics, and how robust is the defense pipeline? A: Sureoush Salahian, CEO: The TOP5 OEM program is a major validation for transitioning from time of flight to FMCW technology. The OEM has extensive experience with time of flight solutions and is now adopting our technology for future-proofing their AV stack. In defense, the market is growing, and our first win with Forterra is a testament to our capabilities. We have several engagements in defense, which could significantly contribute to our near-term sales.

Story Continues

Q: How does the partnership with LG Innotech help in tapping the global physical AI market? A: Sureoush Salahian, CEO: LG Innotech is a key partner in expanding into the physical AI market. They have the scale, resources, and commitment to bring new products based on FMCW technology to market. Our joint product, Omni, is a 360-degree sensor solution for industrial robotics, and we're working on next-generation products to address other markets.

Q: Why is there increased interest from defense customers now, and how does the competitive landscape look? A: Sureoush Salahian, CEO: The defense budget is growing, and there's a shift towards AI-based solutions. Our technology offers advantages like motion estimation and odometry for GPS-denied environments, long-range sensing, and invisibility to night vision goggles. These features are driving interest from defense customers, and we've already made significant shipments in this sector.

Q: With recent OEM pullbacks on Level 3 deployments, do you foresee any impact on existing programs or pipeline delays? A: Sureoush Salahian, CEO: Some OEMs are transitioning to future-proof their stacks, which aligns with our technology. Our Level 3 program with a TOP10 passenger OEM is a result of their transition from time of flight solutions. OEMs are focusing on achieving Level 3 capabilities by 2028-2029, and our technology is well-positioned to support these timelines.

For the complete transcript of the earnings call, please refer to the full earnings call transcript.

title: Aeva Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
content:
Awarded Major Global Production Contract from Top 10 Passenger OEM

Selected by Top 5 Passenger OEM for Global Vehicle Development Program

Nvidia Selected Aeva as Reference LiDAR Sensor for DRIVE Hyperion Platform

Doubled Revenue in 2025 Driven by Scaling Volume and New Markets Expansion

MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif., February 26, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Aeva® (Nasdaq: AEVA), a leader in next-generation sensing and perception systems, today announced its fourth quarter and full year 2025 results.

Key Company Highlights

A top European passenger OEM awarded Aeva a major production contract as the exclusive Tier-1 LiDAR supplier for the OEM’s global L3 production program outside of China with targeted SOP in 2028 New top 5 passenger OEM selected Aeva for a joint development program for its global vehicle lineup across multiple car brands, with opportunity for a large scale production program award Nvidia selected Aeva 4D LiDAR as the reference sensor for its DRIVE Hyperion platform used by leading automotive OEMs and AV companies to enable L3 and higher automated driving Successfully completed on-road validation of Aeva Atlas B-samples for Daimler Truck and on track to deliver C-samples in 2026 as the exclusive long-range LiDAR and primary detection sensor for Daimler Truck’s series production autonomous trucks Secured first win in the defense market with Forterra’s selection of Aeva 4D LiDAR to enable autonomous defense vehicles Introduced Omni, the first compact wide-view short-range FMCW LiDAR targeting a broad range of physical AI applications Delivered key 2025 company objectives, including converting significant commercial momentum to multiple wins, scaling manufacturing and achieving record revenue while strengthening balance sheet and reducing operating expenses

"2025 was a landmark year for Aeva, marked by accelerating demand for our unique perception platform from a growing list of customers and strategic partners across broad applications," said Soroush, Salehian, Co-founder and CEO at Aeva. "This culminated in our first series production award with a top 10 passenger OEM, and we began 2026 expanding our leadership position with a new development program with a top 5 passenger OEM and our first win in defense with Forterra for autonomous ground vehicles. We expect Aeva’s momentum to continue, with additional production program opportunities, scaling manufacturing to meet our customers’ needs and delivering another record revenue year."

Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Highlights

Cash, Cash Equivalents and Marketable Securities

Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities of $121.9 million and available facility of $125.0 million as of December 31, 2025 Revenue

Revenue of $5.6 million in Q4 2025, compared to revenue of $2.7 million in Q4 2024 Revenue of $18.1 million for full year 2025, compared to revenue of $9.1 million for full year 2024 GAAP and Non-GAAP Operating Loss*

GAAP operating loss of $29.1 million in Q4 2025, compared to GAAP operating loss of $34.2 million in Q4 2024 GAAP operating loss of $127.6 million for full year 2025, compared to GAAP operating loss of $158.4 million for full year 2024 Non-GAAP operating loss of $23.8 million in Q4 2025, compared to non-GAAP operating loss of $27.7 million in Q4 2024 Non-GAAP operating loss of $102.0 million for full year 2025, compared to non-GAAP operating loss of $123.2 million for full year 2024 GAAP and Non-GAAP Net Loss per Share*

GAAP net loss per share of $0.42 in Q4 2025, compared to GAAP net loss per share of $0.67 in Q4 2024 GAAP net loss per share of $2.55 for full year 2025, compared to GAAP net loss per share of $2.85 for full year 2024 Non-GAAP net loss per share of $0.40 in Q4 2025, compared to non-GAAP net loss per share of $0.49 in Q4 2024 Non-GAAP net loss per share of $1.75 for full year 2025, compared to non-GAAP net loss per share of $2.17 for full year 2024 Shares Outstanding

Weighted average shares outstanding of 60.2 million in Q4 2025 and 57.0 million for full year 2025

Story Continues

2026 Financial Outlook

Revenue in the range of $30 million to $36 million, representing growth of approximately 70% to 100% year-over-year

*Tables reconciling GAAP to non-GAAP measures are provided at the end of this release.

Conference Call Details

Aeva will host a conference call and live webcast to discuss results at 2:00 p.m. PT / 5:00 p.m. ET today, February 26, 2026. The live webcast and replay can be accessed at investors.aeva.com.

About Aeva Technologies, Inc. (Nasdaq: AEVA)

Aeva’s mission is to bring the next wave of perception to a broad range of applications from automated driving, manufacturing automation and smart infrastructure, to robotics and consumer devices. Aeva is accelerating autonomy with its groundbreaking perception platform that integrates lidar-on-chip technology, system-on-chip processing, and perception algorithms onto silicon leveraging silicon photonics. Aeva 4D LiDAR sensors uniquely detect velocity and position simultaneously, allowing automated devices like vehicles and robots to make more intelligent and safe decisions. For more information, visit www.aeva.com, or connect with us on X or LinkedIn.

Aeva, the Aeva logo, Aeva 4D LiDAR, Aeva Atlas, Aeries, Aeva Eve, Aeva Omni, Aeva Ultra Resolution, Aeva CoreVision, and Aeva X1 are trademarks/registered trademarks of Aeva, Inc. All rights reserved. Third-party trademarks are the property of their respective owners.

title: Catalyst Watch: Amazon earnings, IPO blitz, gold swings, and the jobs report
content:
[bad day at wall street]
franckreporter

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - FEBRUARY 2

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Coinbase Global (COIN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COIN]) and Reddit (RDDT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDDT]) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Goldsky Resources (GSKRF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSKRF]), Amerigo Resources (ARREF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARREF]), and Precipitate Gold (PREIF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PREIF]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Roper (ROP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ROP]), Commerce.com (CMRC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMRC]), and Flutter Entertainment (FLUT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FLUT]). Traders also expect more outsized swings for spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XAUUSD:CUR]) (GLD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GLD]) and silver (XAGUSD:CUR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XAGUSD:CUR]) (SLV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SLV]). Warner Bros Discovery (WBD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WBD]) and Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]) could be active with the Senate antitrust hearing set. 
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Palantir (PLTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PLTR]), Disney (DIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DIS]), NXP Semiconductors (NXPI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NXPI]), Tyson Foods (TSN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TSN]), Rambus (RMBS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RMBS]), and Teradyne (TER [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TER]).
 	* IPO WATCH - A busy week for IPOs will see the trading debuts for VeraDermics (MANE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MANE]), Once Upon a Farm (OFRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OFRM]), Eikon Therapeutics (EIKN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EIKN]), Bob's Discount Furniture (BOBS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BOBS]), Liftoff Mobile (LFTO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LFTO]), and Forgent Power Solutions (FPS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FPS]). The IPO lockup period expires for blocks of shares of Firefly Aerospace (FLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FLY]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Citigroup (C [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/C]), MetLife (MET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MET]), Valero (VLO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VLO]), and JB Hunt (JBHT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JBHT]).
 	* OIL WATCH - Oil futures could be active after eight OPEC+ nations reviewed the decision to pause oil production increases in Q1.
 	* ALL DAY - Lululemon (LULU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LULU]) CFO Meghan Frank and Chief Commercial Officer André Maestrini will start to share leadership duties as co-CEOs while the company continues its search for a full-time replacement for Calvin McDonald.
 	* ALL DAY - The National Automobile Dealers Association’s annual four-day convention in Las Vegas begins. The event brings together dealers, manufacturers, and technology vendors. Marchex (MCHX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCHX]), Faraday Future Intelligent Electric (FFAI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FFAI]), and Pinewood.AI (PINWF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PINWF]) are some of the companies participating.
 	* 2:00 P.M. The Federal Reserve's Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey on Bank Lending Practices will be released. The SLOOS report will provide an update on the conditions, supply and demand of bank loans extended to customers in the U.S. Tightening standards can signal stress in the banking system or broader economy, impacting financial stocks.

TUESDAY - FEBRUARY 3

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include AMD (AMD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMD]), Merck (MRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRK]), PepsiCo (PEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PEP]), Amgen (AMGN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMGN]), Suncor Energy (SU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SU]), Mondelez International (MDLZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDLZ]), Electronic Arts (EA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EA]), PayPal (PYPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PYPL]), and Chipotle (CMG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMG]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Galaxy Digital (GLXY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GLXY]) and fuboTV (FUBO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FUBO]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Western Digital Corporation (WDC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDC]) will hold its Innovation Day event in New York City. At the event, the company will highlight key innovations designed to support the growing demand for data storage by customers in the AI-driven data economy.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Semrush (SEMR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SEMR]) vote on the acquisition offer from Adobe (ADBE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ADBE]). Semrush traded at less than 1% from the deal price at the time of publication.
 	* 8:15 A.M. PepsiCo (PEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PEP]) will hold its earnings conference call. Key topics will include the guidance update, organic revenue and volume trends, North America snacks and beverages elasticity, and execution on the new shareholder-value plan. Key suppliers Celsius Holdings (CELH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CELH]) and McCormick (MKC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MKC]) trade directionally with PepsiCo about 75% of the time after earnings.
 	* 10:30 A.M. The two-day Oppenheimer Emerging Growth Conference will begin. Aeva Technologies (AEVA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AEVA]), GrowGeneration (GRWG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GRWG]), and Aehr Test Systems (AEHR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AEHR]) are some of the notable companies participating.

WEDNESDAY - FEBRUARY 4

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Alphabet (GOOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOG]), Eli Lilly (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]), AbbVie (ABBV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABBV]), Uber Technologies (UBER [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/UBER]), and Qualcomm (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Boot Barn (BOOT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BOOT]) and Snap (SNAP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNAP]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Noodles & Company (NDLS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NDLS]) vote on a proposed reverse stock split.
 	* ALL DAY - General Motors (GM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GM]) CFO Paul Jacobson will give comments at the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago Automotive Insights Symposium.
 	* POSTMARKET - Costco (COST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COST]) will issue its January sales report.

THURSDAY - FEBRUARY 5

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Amazon (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]), Shell (SHEL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SHEL]), ConocoPhillips (COP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COP]), Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMY]), Barrick Mining (B [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/B]), and ArcelorMittal (MT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MT]). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Reddit (RDDT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDDT]), Gold.com (Gold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOLD]), and Roblox (RBLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RBLX]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - American Axle & Manufacturing (AXL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AXL]) will begin trading under its new name and symbol of Dauch Corporation (DCH). The name change is tied to the transformational strategic milestone of its planned acquisition of Dowlais Group and to reposition the business under a broader, less product-specific corporate identity.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Veeco Instruments (VECO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VECO]) and Axcelis Technologies (ACLS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ACLS]) vote on the planned merger between the companies.
 	* 7:45 A.M. The European Central Bank will release its policy statement.
 	* 8:30 A.M. European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde will hold a press conference.
 	* 10:50 A.M. Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will participate in a moderated conversation and Q&A on monetary policy.
 	* 1:30 P.M. St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem will speak on the U.S. economy and monetary policy and participate in a moderated discussion before the University of Arkansas Center for Business and Economic Research.
 	* 5:00 P.M. Amazon (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]) will hold its earnings conference call. Key topics will include AWS growth and competitive positioning, how investors should view surging AI-driven capex, and 2026 investment plans that could include OpenAI (OPENAI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OPENAI]). Suppliers that have a high trading correlation with Amazon on earnings day include Hamilton Beach (HBB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HBB]), Marvell Technology (MRVL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRVL]), and Aterian (ATER [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ATER]).

FRIDAY - FEBRUARY 6

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Toyota Motor (TM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TM]), Philip Morris (PM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PM]), Centene (CNC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CNC]), and Autonation (AN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AN]).
 	* ALL DAY - Immunovant (IMVT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IMVT]) will hold a business update call.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The January jobs report will be released. Economists expect 65K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to remain at 4.4%.

MORE ON THE MARKETS 

 	* 2026: The Year Of The Small Cap? [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4864427-2026-the-year-of-the-small-cap]
 	* U.S. Trade Deficit Stabilizes As Post-Tariff 'Front-Loading' Fades [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4864474-us-trade-deficit-stabilizes-post-tariff-front-loading-fades]
 	* Evolution, Not Revolution For The Federal Reserve [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4864419-evolution-not-revolution-federal-reserve]
 	* The Mag 7 are no longer trading as a unified group – T. Rowe Price’s Tony Wang [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4544914-the-mag-7-are-no-longer-trading-as-a-unified-group-t-rowe-price-s-tony-wang]
 	* SA analysts share their thoughts on Trump's selection of Kevin Warsh [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4544903-sa-analysts-share-their-thoughts-on-trumps-selection-of-kevin-warsh]

title: Aeva Technologies (AEVA) Is Up 21.8% After NVIDIA DRIVE LiDAR Win And New AI Chip Push – Has The Bull Case Changed?
content:
Aeva Technologies has recently unveiled a high-power semiconductor optical amplifier for AI data centers, launched its Omni 4D LiDAR with LG Innotek, and was selected to supply FMCW 4D LiDAR for NVIDIA’s DRIVE Hyperion platform and a top European automaker’s future autonomous vehicles. Taken together, these moves highlight Aeva’s push to extend its silicon-photonics platform from automotive sensing into broader Physical AI and data center infrastructure. We’ll now explore how Aeva’s selection for NVIDIA’s DRIVE Hyperion platform influences the company’s investment narrative and long-term positioning.

Prediction Market powered by

We've found 12 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

What Is Aeva Technologies' Investment Narrative?

To own Aeva here, you have to believe its silicon‑photonics platform can become a core enabler across autonomous driving, Physical AI and now AI data centers, not just a niche LiDAR play. The NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion win and the top European OEM program were already key long‑term proof points, and the new Omni sensor with LG Innotek reinforces that Aeva has partners capable of industrializing the tech. The latest semiconductor optical amplifier announcement broadens the story into data‑center optics, but on its own it does not change the near‑term picture much: Aeva is still small, unprofitable, richly valued on book value and reliant on design wins that ramp years out. The main shift is that investors can now frame both upside and execution risk across two demanding end markets instead of one.

However, there is one execution risk in particular that current and potential shareholders should think carefully about. Aeva Technologies' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other PerspectivesAEVA 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Twelve Simply Wall St Community fair value views span roughly US$1 to above US$60 per share, underscoring how far apart expectations sit on Aeva’s eventual scale. Set that against a company still loss making with a very high return on equity deficit and depending heavily on turning its NVIDIA and European OEM wins into meaningful production volumes. For anyone following the stock, it is worth weighing those contrasting opinions alongside the emerging data‑center optics angle.

Explore 12 other fair value estimates on Aeva Technologies - why the stock might be worth over 2x more than the current price!

Build Your Own Aeva Technologies Narrative

Disagree with this assessment? Create your own narrative in under 3 minutes - extraordinary investment returns rarely come from following the herd.

Story Continues

A great starting point for your Aeva Technologies research is our analysis highlighting 2 key rewards and 3 important warning signs that could impact your investment decision. Our free Aeva Technologies research report provides a comprehensive fundamental analysis summarized in a single visual - the Snowflake - making it easy to evaluate Aeva Technologies' overall financial health at a glance.

Want Some Alternatives?

Markets shift fast. These stocks won't stay hidden for long. Get the list while it matters:

Uncover the next big thing with financially sound penny stocks that balance risk and reward. Rare earth metals are the new gold rush. Find out which 38 stocks are leading the charge.

We provide commentary based on historical data and analyst forecasts only using an unbiased methodology and our articles are not intended to be financial advice. It does not constitute a recommendation to buy or sell any stock, and does not take account of your objectives, or your financial situation. We aim to bring you long-term focused analysis driven by fundamental data. Note that our analysis may not factor in the latest price-sensitive company announcements or qualitative material. Simply Wall St has no position in any stocks mentioned.

Companies discussed in this article include AEVA.
